octodns/processor/restrict.py.short_description: Processor that restricts record TTLs to configured min/max or an allowed set.
octodns/processor/restrict.py.description:
This module defines a TTL restriction processor for octoDNS’s processing pipeline. It introduces a dedicated exception type, `RestrictionException`, and a `TtlRestrictionFilter` processor that is meant to run during the “process source zone” stage. Its core job is to validate each record in a zone against configured TTL constraints before downstream steps (planning/applying to providers) occur.

The processor’s behavior is controlled by three configuration inputs: `min_ttl`, `max_ttl`, and `allowed_ttls`. When `allowed_ttls` is explicitly configured (non-empty), the processor ignores the min/max range checks and only allows records whose `record.ttl` is a member of `allowed_ttls`. If `allowed_ttls` is not provided (or is falsy), it enforces `min_ttl` and `max_ttl` bounds for every record. The class also respects record-level leniency: if `record.lenient` is truthy, the processor skips TTL validation for that record.

Within the broader system, this module plugs into the processor framework via inheritance from `BaseProcessor` and an override of `process_source_zone`. That method receives the zone object plus source-related context and returns the zone after validation. Raising `RestrictionException` is the mechanism by which the pipeline can fail early when TTL constraints are violated.

key_constructs.name: RestrictionException
key_constructs.purpose: Specialized processor exception raised when a record TTL violates configured restrictions.
key_constructs.reasoning: Using a dedicated exception type lets the pipeline distinguish restriction failures from other processor errors.

key_constructs.name: TtlRestrictionFilter
key_constructs.purpose: Validates each record’s TTL against configured min/max TTLs or an allowed TTL set.
key_constructs.reasoning: Implements the processor contract by validating zone records during `process_source_zone` and raising `RestrictionException` on violations.

key_constructs.name: process_source_zone
key_constructs.purpose: Iterates zone records and enforces TTL restrictions unless record is marked lenient.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the integration point used by the octoDNS processor pipeline; changes here affect when and how validation occurs.

key_behaviors: Skips TTL validation for records with record.lenient enabled
Enforces TTL membership in allowed_ttls when configured
Enforces TTL lower and upper bounds when allowed_ttls is not configured
Raises RestrictionException with record FQDN and constraint details on violations
Returns the validated zone to allow downstream processing

pitfalls.mistake: Treating min/max checks as always active even when allowed_ttls is configured
pitfalls.consequence: Violations may be reported or missed incorrectly relative to the documented precedence (allowed_ttls should override min/max).
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying logic, preserve the condition that only applies min/max when allowed_ttls is not set (i.e., allowed_ttls is None).

pitfalls.mistake: Ignoring record-level leniency (record.lenient) and validating all records unconditionally
pitfalls.consequence: Configured lenient records that should bypass restrictions could cause pipeline failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure the early `if record.lenient: continue` behavior remains effective and evaluated before TTL checks.

pitfalls.mistake: Allowing allowed_ttls to be treated as a falsy/empty container in an inconsistent way
pitfalls.consequence: If allowed_ttls becomes an empty set unexpectedly, the processor will fall back to min/max behavior, changing semantics.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the initialization semantics: `allowed_ttls` is converted to a set only when the input is truthy; otherwise `self.allowed_ttls` should remain None.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing exception message structure without checking downstream expectations
pitfalls.consequence: Test assertions or log parsing might fail if they rely on the message contents.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the general inclusion of `record.fqdn`, the actual ttl, and the relevant constraint parameters in the raised message.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the ordering of validation checks
pitfalls.consequence: A record failing multiple constraints could raise a different error type/message than expected by users/tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the current precedence: allowed_ttls check first, then min_ttl, then max_ttl.

key_sections: TtlRestrictionFilter.__init__: how min_ttl/max_ttl/allowed_ttls are stored and how allowed_ttls precedence is established
TtlRestrictionFilter.process_source_zone: the validation loop, leniency skip, and exception raising precedence
skip_unless_needed: Docstring example blocks (informational only; not part of runtime behavior)

related_files.relationship: Defines the `BaseProcessor` interface/contract and `ProcessorException` base class that `TtlRestrictionFilter` and `RestrictionException` integrate with.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the processor pipeline changes the signature/expectations of `process_source_zone` or how exceptions are handled, this processor must remain compatible.

related_files.relationship: Defines the `record` object attributes used here (`record.ttl`, `record.fqdn`, `record.lenient`).
related_files.reason_to_check: If TTL types or lenient semantics change, this restriction logic’s comparisons and membership checks could behave differently.

related_files.relationship: Defines the `zone.records` iteration target used in the processor loop.
related_files.reason_to_check: Only relevant if record enumeration or zone structure changes in ways that affect which records are validated.

octodns/record/srv.py.short_description: Implements SRV record value parsing/validation and SRV target templating.
octodns/record/srv.py.description:
This module defines how SRV record values are represented, compared, validated, and rendered. The core abstraction is `SrvValue`, a dict-like value object that stores `priority`, `weight`, `port`, and `target`. It integrates with the record framework via `SrvRecord`, which declares `_type = 'SRV'` and uses `SrvValue` as `_value_type`.

`SrvValue` supports several lifecycle hooks used by the broader record parsing/processing pipeline: `parse_rdata_text` parses a single SRV rdata string into structured fields; `validate` verifies required keys exist and that `priority`, `weight`, and `port` are integer-coercible, and that `target` passes `validate_target_fqdn` from `octodns/record/target.py`. The constructor (`__init__`) performs the actual type coercion and IDNA encoding of the target via `idna_encode`. For presentation, `rdata_text` rebuilds the SRV rdata line, and `__repr__`/`__hash__` rely on the equality tuple used by `EqualityTupleMixin`.

A key non-obvious behavior is templating: `SrvValue.template(params)` only applies formatting when the `target` contains a `{` placeholder. It creates a new `SrvValue` instance and substitutes `target.format(**params)`; otherwise it returns `self` (identity). This matches the intent that templating should be a no-op unless placeholders are present. SRV record name validation is also customized via `_name_re`, allowing either a leading `*` label or an underscore-prefixed label fragment as the first part of the record name.

key_constructs.name: SrvValue
key_constructs.purpose: Dict-based SRV value object with parse/validate/render and templating for the target.
key_constructs.reasoning: Editors must preserve the contract expected by the record framework: type coercion in `__init__`, schema checks in `validate`, stable equality via `_equality_tuple`, and templating identity/no-op behavior.

key_constructs.name: parse_rdata_text
key_constructs.purpose: Convert SRV rdata text (priority/weight/port/target) into a dict of fields.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is used when ingesting provider text formats; it must raise `RrParseError` on wrong token counts, and it intentionally tolerates non-integer tokens (casts are deferred/validated later).

key_constructs.name: validate
key_constructs.purpose: Validate an iterable of SRV value dicts and return a list of human-readable reasons.
key_constructs.reasoning: The test suite asserts exact reason strings (e.g., 'missing priority', 'invalid priority "foo"'), so changes to error formatting or ordering can break tests.

key_constructs.name: template
key_constructs.purpose: Apply `.format(**params)` substitutions to `target` only when `{` is present.
key_constructs.reasoning: Both identity behavior (returns `self` when no placeholders) and field restriction (only `target` is templated) are part of the implied API exercised by tests in `tests/test_octodns_record_srv.py`.

key_constructs.name: SrvRecord
key_constructs.purpose: Record wrapper for SRV that wires `SrvValue` into the generic record system and validates record names.
key_constructs.reasoning: SRV record validation extends base validation by checking `_name_re` and otherwise defers to `ValuesMixin`/`Record` mechanisms.

key_constructs.name: validate
key_constructs.purpose: SRV-specific record name validation merged with parent class validation.
key_constructs.reasoning: The method prepends 'invalid name for SRV record' when `_name_re` fails, and the tests assert exact `ValidationError.reasons` contents.

key_behaviors: parses SRV rdata text into structured value dicts
coerces priority/weight/port to int and IDNA-encodes target in `__init__`
validates integer coercibility and target FQDN rules via `validate_target_fqdn`
renders SRV rdata text from value fields
applies template formatting to `target` only when `{` is present (no-op returns same instance)

pitfalls.consequence: Unit tests in `tests/test_octodns_record_srv.py` assert exact lists of reasons; mismatches cause failures even if logic is correct.
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying validation logic, verify against the exact expected strings and list ordering in the SRV record tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Templating more fields than intended (or templating target even when there are no placeholders)
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks the no-op identity expectation (`value.template(...)` returns `self` when `{` is not present) and could trigger unintended changes downstream.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the conditional `{` check on `target` and ensure only `target` is formatted.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering `idna_encode` behavior for `target`
pitfalls.consequence: Targets with internationalized characters may fail equality/hash comparisons and/or provider rendering consistency.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure any edits keep the encoding step in `__init__`.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the `_name_re` pattern semantics
pitfalls.consequence: SRV record name validation behavior changes; tests validate wildcard permission and rejection of invalid names.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep `_name_re` behavior consistent with SRV record naming expectations verified by tests.

Exhaustive `Record.new` SRV validation reason assertions: missing/invalid fields, name validation, and target FQDN checks

related_files.reason_to_check: If SRV validation failures or templating-time behavior differ, the source of truth is `validate_target_fqdn`.

related_files.reason_to_check: If editing shared record-value conventions, compare against MX for consistent templating/no-op and hashing behavior.

insights.title: Implement/verify templating for SRV target
insights.problem: SRV `target` must support template placeholders for correct downstream resolution.
insights.root_cause: Templating contract needed consistent behavior for target-like fields.
insights.solution: Added tests verifying noop identity when no `{` exists and correct formatting of `target` when placeholders exist.
insights.lesson_learned: Target-like fields should follow a common template/noop contract to reduce inconsistent behavior across record types.

tests/test_octodns_record_srv.py.short_description: Unit tests for SRV record/value parsing, validation, equality, and templating.
tests/test_octodns_record_srv.py.description:
This test module verifies the behavior of SRV record and SRV value objects in `octodns/record/srv.py`. It exercises the SRV-specific record name validation, value field parsing (including `RrParseError` behavior), conversion to structured values, equality/ordering/hash behavior through `EqualityTupleMixin`, change detection via `Record.changes`, and templating contract.

The tests use the generic `Record` factory (`Record.new`) to ensure SRV data schema validation integrates properly with the rest of the record framework. They also construct `SrvRecord`/`SrvValue` directly to verify field coercion and rdata rendering. For templating, it uses `octodns.processor.templating.Templating` and a lenient/non-lenient configuration to ensure placeholder substitution rules are enforced in the record framework, while `SrvValue.template` itself only substitutes the `target` field.

Because many assertions compare exact reason strings and rely on list equality, this file is sensitive to any modifications that affect validation output formatting or ordering. It also checks for identity semantics in templating (the `template` method returns the same instance when no placeholders are present).

key_constructs.name: TestRecordSrv
key_constructs.purpose: Primary SRV record/value unit tests for parsing, validation, changes, and rdata text rendering.
key_constructs.reasoning: This suite defines the behavioral contract for SRV record construction and all core value transformations.

key_constructs.name: TestSrvValue
key_constructs.purpose: Focused tests for `SrvValue.template` behavior (identity and substitution).
key_constructs.reasoning: These assertions constrain templating semantics and instance allocation behavior.

key_constructs.name: test_srv
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies SRV record fields, values array shape, equality/change detection, and `__repr__` safety.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures `SrvValue` properties are exposed and `Record.changes` reacts to specific field diffs.

key_constructs.name: test_srv_value_rdata_text
key_constructs.purpose: Validates SRV rdata parsing error handling and rdata text reconstruction for quoted targets.
key_constructs.reasoning: Locks down `parse_rdata_text` token count behavior and integration with `unquote`.

octodns/cmds/args.py.short_description: CLI argument parsing with logging configuration and verbosity controls.
octodns/cmds/args.py.description:
This module defines a custom `ArgumentParser` used by octoDNS CLI commands. Its primary responsibilities are to (1) register common CLI flags for version output and logging behavior, (2) parse arguments, and (3) initialize Python logging based on either a provided YAML logging config or built-in defaults (stderr/stout plus optional syslog output).

The parser adds flags such as `--version`, `--log-stream-stdout`, `--log-syslog`, `--syslog-device`, `--syslog-facility`, `--debug`, `--quiet`, and `--logging-config`. After parsing, it calls `_setup_logging(args, default_log_level)`, which selects one of two paths: if `args.logging_config` is set, it loads the YAML file via `octodns.yaml.safe_load(..., enforce_order=False)` and applies it using `logging.config.dictConfig`, then returns without applying the module’s own handler/level configuration. Otherwise it creates a `StreamHandler` targeting either `stdout` or `stderr`, optionally adds a `SysLogHandler`, sets the root logger level based on `default_log_level` or the `--debug`/`--quiet` overrides, and ensures the `Plan` logger level is INFO even when quiet mode is enabled.

This file fits into the larger system by establishing the logging environment that other octoDNS components depend on for runtime behavior and user-facing output (notably “plan” output). Because logging configuration can be overridden entirely via `--logging-config`, it also acts as a central compatibility point: other parts of the codebase should assume that logging may be controlled externally and that this module will either apply its standard setup or defer entirely to the YAML-provided configuration.

key_constructs.name: ArgumentParser
key_constructs.purpose: Subclass of argparse’s ArgumentParser with octoDNS-specific CLI flags and logging setup logic.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulating argument registration and logging initialization ensures every CLI entrypoint can share consistent behavior around verbosity, handlers, and syslog support.

key_constructs.name: parse_args
key_constructs.purpose: Adds standard octoDNS CLI/logging options, parses CLI args, and triggers `_setup_logging`.
key_constructs.reasoning: The method centralizes both user-facing CLI surface area and side effects (logging initialization) so callers can rely on logging being configured immediately after parsing.

key_constructs.name: _setup_logging
key_constructs.purpose: Configures logging either from a YAML config file (`dictConfig`) or from built-in stream/syslog handlers and level overrides.
key_constructs.reasoning: The dual-path design (external YAML config vs internal default setup) is critical because the rest of the application must work under both configurations without double-setting handlers or levels.

key_behaviors: Registers common flags for version output and logging behavior.
If `--logging-config` is provided, loads YAML and applies it via `dictConfig` then skips built-in logging setup.
Otherwise creates a `StreamHandler` targeting stdout or stderr based on `--log-stream-stdout`.
Optionally adds a `SysLogHandler` using `--syslog-device` and `--syslog-facility` when `--log-syslog` is set.
Sets root logger level based on `default_log_level`, then overrides with DEBUG when `--debug` is set or WARNING when `--quiet` is set.
When `--quiet` is set, explicitly sets the `Plan` logger level to INFO to keep plan output available.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming `_setup_logging` always applies the built-in handlers/levels.
pitfalls.consequence: If `--logging-config` is used, the method returns early after `dictConfig`, so any expectations about default handlers, syslog enablement, or `Plan` logger level may not hold.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing behavior or debugging logging, confirm whether `args.logging_config` is set and inspect the YAML config’s effect.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the `Plan` logger behavior without accounting for quiet mode.
pitfalls.consequence: Plan output may disappear or be less visible when users use `--quiet` because the code explicitly adjusts `getLogger('Plan').setLevel(INFO)` only in that branch.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the quiet-mode semantics where plan output remains enabled at INFO.

pitfalls.mistake: Adding handlers repeatedly without considering root logger handler accumulation.
pitfalls.consequence: If `parse_args/_setup_logging` is invoked more than once per process, `logger.addHandler(...)` will add duplicate handlers, resulting in repeated log lines.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure any calling code only configures logging once per process lifecycle; when editing this module, be mindful that it currently does not clear existing handlers.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the syslog address/facility defaults or their argument names.
pitfalls.consequence: Users or scripts may rely on current defaults (`/dev/log`, `local0`) and CLI option names; mismatches can break syslog output.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep CLI flag names and default values consistent unless the CLI contract is deliberately changed.

reading_guide.start_here: ArgumentParser.parse_args (it registers CLI options and triggers _setup_logging)
key_sections: parse_args: where CLI flags are defined and arguments are parsed
_setup_logging: where logging is configured (YAML dictConfig path vs built-in handler path)
skip_unless_needed: The minimal `__init__` override (it just calls `super().__init__` with no added behavior)

related_files.relationship: Provides `safe_load` used to load YAML logging config with `enforce_order=False`.
related_files.reason_to_check: If YAML parsing/enforce_order behavior changes, it can impact logging config loading compatibility in this module.

related_files.relationship: Likely constructs and uses `ArgumentParser` and passes `default_log_level`.
related_files.reason_to_check: Ensure any edits to logging/argument semantics still match how entrypoints call `parse_args`.

related_files.relationship: Other modules log to the `Plan` logger name, which this module adjusts for quiet mode.
related_files.reason_to_check: If logger names or plan logging behavior are changed elsewhere, quiet-mode behavior in this module must remain consistent with those conventions.

octodns/record/chunked.py.short_description: Chunking + validation helpers for TXT-like record values.
octodns/record/chunked.py.description:
This file defines shared logic used by TXT/SPF-like record value types that must be represented as multiple DNS string chunks. The primary abstraction is `_ChunkedValuesMixin`, which extends `ValuesMixin` and provides `chunked_value()` plus properties that expose the chunked representation via `chunked_values` and `rr_values`.

The chunking algorithm in `chunked_value()` is responsible for producing a canonical DNS-ready rdata text form. It escapes embedded double quotes, splits the input into pieces sized by `CHUNK_SIZE` (default 255), and then adjusts chunk boundaries to avoid splitting when the cut point would cause an escape sequence to be broken (it reduces the chunk length while the character immediately before the end would be a backslash). After chunking, it joins the chunk segments using the literal quote-space-quote delimiter (`" "`) and wraps the joined string using the configured `_value_type`.

The `_ChunkedValue` class is a string subclass intended to model a chunked TXT-like value. It supports `parse_rdata_text()` (escapes semicolons for raw rdata text inputs), `validate()` (returns a list of exact reason strings for missing values, unescaped semicolons vs double-escaped semicolons, and non-ASCII input), `process()` (normalizes processed values by stripping outer quotes and removing the exact join delimiter sequence), and `template()` (formats `{...}` placeholders only when present, returning `self` unchanged when there are none). These behaviors are directly asserted by `tests/test_octodns_record_chunked.py` as a behavioral contract.

key_constructs.name: _ChunkedValuesMixin
key_constructs.purpose: Mixin that chunk-splits long TXT-like strings into provider/rr-ready rdata text values.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes canonical chunk encoding rules and the mapping from internal `values` to external `rr_values`.

key_constructs.name: chunked_value
key_constructs.purpose: Split one logical string into DNS-sized quoted chunks with escape-aware boundaries.
key_constructs.reasoning: Defines the canonical escaping, boundary adjustment, chunk joining, and wrapping as `_value_type`.

key_constructs.name: chunked_values
key_constructs.purpose: Build a list of chunked typed values corresponding to `self.values`.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures downstream code receives `_value_type` instances rather than raw strings.

key_constructs.name: rr_values
key_constructs.purpose: Expose chunked values for rr/provider serialization paths.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used as an indirection hook so serialization sees the chunked rdata text form.

key_constructs.name: _ChunkedValue
key_constructs.purpose: Typed string wrapper for chunked TXT-like rdata values with parsing/validation/normalization/template support.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates TXT/SPF-specific constraints (escaping + ASCII) and ensures consistent normalization with chunk join formatting.

key_constructs.name: parse_rdata_text
key_constructs.purpose: Escape semicolons in raw rdata text inputs (`;` -> `\;`).
key_constructs.reasoning: Tolerates non-string inputs by catching `AttributeError` and returning the input unchanged.

key_constructs.name: validate
key_constructs.purpose: Validate chunked values for missing input, semicolon escaping correctness, and ASCII-only encoding.
key_constructs.reasoning: Returns exact reason strings; tests assert those exact messages.

key_constructs.name: process
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize processed values by removing surrounding quotes and the join delimiter sequence.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must match how `chunked_value()` formats the canonical representation so round-tripping works.

key_constructs.name: template
key_constructs.purpose: Apply string templating with `.format(**params)` when placeholders exist.
key_constructs.reasoning: Returns `self` when no `{` placeholders are present to preserve identity semantics expected by tests.

key_behaviors: Escapes embedded double quotes before chunking
Splits into segments of length `CHUNK_SIZE` with boundary adjustment to avoid breaking escape sequences ending in backslash
Joins chunk segments using delimiter `" "` and wraps the result using `_value_type`
Exposes chunked typed values via `chunked_values` and `rr_values`
Validates missing values, unescaped semicolons, double-escaped semicolons, and non-ASCII content with exact reason strings
Parses raw rdata text by escaping semicolons for string inputs
Normalizes processed values by stripping outer quotes and removing the exact join delimiter sequence
Templates via `.format(**params)` when `{` exists; otherwise returns `self` unchanged

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the chunk join delimiter without synchronizing `_ChunkedValue.process()` normalization
pitfalls.consequence: Round-trip behavior (chunked_value output -> process normalization -> final rdata_text) can become inconsistent.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep delimiter logic in sync with tests’ exact expected chunk strings.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying validation regex matching or reason string formatting
pitfalls.consequence: Unit tests will fail due to exact string assertions, and downstream validation reporting may differ.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat `validate()` reason strings and regex intent as a strict contract.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the identity fast path in `_ChunkedValue.template()`
pitfalls.consequence: Tests use `assertIs` to require identity preservation when no placeholders exist.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the current `{`-presence check behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the boundary-safe splitting logic around backslashes
pitfalls.consequence: Escaped sequences can be split across chunks incorrectly, producing wrong DNS encoding in edge cases.
pitfalls.prevention: Re-run `TestChunkedValue.test_splitting` after any boundary-related edits.

key_sections: chunked_value: escape quotes, determine chunk size, adjust boundary when preceding char is backslash, join with `" "`, wrap via `_value_type`
_ChunkedValue: parse_rdata_text, validate (regexes + ascii), process (strip quotes + remove delimiter), template (noop identity vs formatted new instance)
skip_unless_needed: The small regex definitions themselves (only relevant if validation patterns are changed).

relevant_snippets.description: parse_rdata_text semicolon escaping and SpfRecord rdata_text integration expectation.
Exact validate() reason strings for missing, unescaped ;, double escaped ;, and non-ASCII.
Exact expected outputs for chunk splitting boundary and escape spanning cases.
template noop identity (assertIs) and formatted new instance behavior.

related_files.relationship: Direct unit tests specifying exact encoding/validation/template semantics.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any semantic change in chunking or validation must be reflected in these expectations.

insights.title: Add double-escaped semicolon validation for chunked TXT values
insights.problem: TXT chunked values could contain malformed escaping like double-escaped semicolons and were not flagged.
insights.root_cause: Validation only looked for unescaped semicolons via `_unescaped_semicolon_re` but didn’t check for `\\;` (double-escaped semicolons).
insights.solution: Introduced `_double_escaped_semicolon_re = re.compile(r'\\;')` and added a corresponding validation reason `double escaped ; in "..."` in `_ChunkedValue.validate`.
insights.lesson_learned: Escaping-related validation should explicitly distinguish between “unescaped” vs “double-escaped” sequences, and tests should assert exact reason strings to avoid drift.

insights.title: Implement Value.template for chunked values
insights.problem: Chunked string values needed templating support so record value templates could be applied consistently.
insights.root_cause: Only some value classes had `template`; chunked values lacked a consistent mechanism.
insights.solution: Added `_ChunkedValue.template(self, params)` that returns `self` as a noop when there are no `{` placeholders, otherwise returns a newly formatted instance.
insights.lesson_learned: Template methods should follow a consistent contract: noop (return identical object) when no placeholders exist, otherwise return a new typed value with formatted fields.

insights.title: Add ASCII-only validation to chunked DNS value type
insights.problem: ChunkedValue could accept non-ASCII characters, leading to invalid/unexpected DNS record encodings.
insights.root_cause: No explicit ASCII enforcement before processing/serialization.
insights.solution: Added unicode->ascii check: encode('ascii') and report 'non ASCII character in ...' validation failure; existing validation already caught unescaped semicolons.
insights.lesson_learned: For DNS wire-format constraints, validate encoding assumptions early (ASCII/escaping) and add tests for representative Unicode inputs.

insights.title: chunked_values should return _value_type wrapper instead of plain strings
insights.problem: Returned chunked_values were plain strings, which could break downstream expectations that chunked values behave like the typed _value_type objects.
insights.root_cause: In chunked values rendering, wrapping behavior was inconsistent: _ChunkedValuesMixin built a quoted string but returned it directly.
insights.solution: Changed return to return self._value_type(f'"{vs}"') so values preserve the expected type semantics.
insights.lesson_learned: Be careful to preserve wrapper/value types in serialization-related helpers; unit tests should cover both value equality and type expectations.

insights.title: ValuesMixin.rrs supports chunked TXT values via rr_values indirection
insights.problem: The built-in rrs property used self.values, but for chunked TXT records the provider-facing representation should use chunked_values when chunking is active.
insights.root_cause: rrs generation didn’t account for alternate underlying value representations (chunked values).
insights.solution: Added rr_values() hook to ValuesMixin and _ChunkedValuesMixin override to return chunked_values. Updated rrs property to use [v.rdata_text for v in self.rr_values()].
insights.lesson_learned: When supporting multiple internal value representations (raw vs chunked), introduce an explicit hook method rather than trying to infer representation from value types.

tests/test_octodns_record_chunked.py.short_description: Tests chunked TXT/SPF parsing, validation, boundary splitting, and templating.
tests/test_octodns_record_chunked.py.description:
This test suite defines the required behavior for chunked TXT-like value helpers from `octodns.record.chunked`. It validates both low-level helper behavior and one small integration behavior using `SpfRecord` and a `Zone` instance.

`TestRecordChunked.test_chunked_value_rdata_text` checks that `_ChunkedValue.parse_rdata_text` is tolerant (returns inputs unchanged for `None` and non-string values) and that for string inputs semicolons are escaped into `\;`. It also asserts that a `SpfRecord` stores/reflects the value’s `rdata_text` such that it preserves the original SPF-like string.

`TestChunkedValue` covers `validate()`, chunk splitting, and templating. `test_validate` asserts exact reason lists for missing values, unescaped semicolons, double-escaped semicolons, and non-ASCII characters (ASCII enforcement via `encode('ascii')`). `test_splitting` is the core encoding specification: it introduces a local `SmallerChunkedMixin` that sets `CHUNK_SIZE = 8` to force frequent boundaries, and asserts exact chunked outputs for many edge cases involving escapes at/before/after/spanning boundaries. `test_template` verifies identity/no-op behavior when no `{` placeholders exist and new formatted-instance behavior when placeholders are present.

key_constructs.name: TestRecordChunked
key_constructs.purpose: Validates parse_rdata_text behavior and small integration with SpfRecord.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures helper parsing rules align with record value rdata_text behavior.

key_constructs.name: TestChunkedValue
key_constructs.purpose: Specifies validate(), chunk boundary splitting, and template identity/formatting behavior.
key_constructs.reasoning: Assertions are exact-string and identity-based, so they function as precise behavioral contracts.

key_constructs.name: SmallerChunkedMixin
key_constructs.purpose: Test helper mixin that reduces CHUNK_SIZE to simplify deterministic boundary testing.
key_constructs.reasoning: Forces boundary conditions that would otherwise require long input strings.

key_behaviors: Ensures parse_rdata_text escapes semicolons for string inputs and tolerates non-string inputs
Ensures validate() returns exact reason strings for semicolon escaping and ASCII validation failures
Ensures chunk splitting output formatting is exact across escape boundary scenarios
Ensures template() preserves identity when no placeholders exist and returns a new instance when formatting applies

pitfalls.mistake: Changing helper behavior without updating tests’ exact expected strings and reason lists
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will fail due to strict equality assertions over encoded chunk strings and validation reason text.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat expected outputs as the canonical encoding spec.

pitfalls.mistake: Breaking identity semantics in template() implementation
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will fail due to `assertIs`/`assertIsNot` checks.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain template noop behavior for strings without `{`.

key_sections: TestRecordChunked.test_chunked_value_rdata_text: parse_rdata_text + SpfRecord rdata_text expectation
TestChunkedValue.test_validate: exact reason strings
TestChunkedValue.test_splitting: boundary/escape spanning expected output table
TestChunkedValue.test_template: identity semantics
skip_unless_needed: Nested Base class (only used to satisfy mixin inheritance for SmallerChunkedMixin).

relevant_snippets.description: parse_rdata_text escape behavior and integration assertion with SpfRecord.
validate() strict reason list assertions.
chunk splitting expected outputs including escape boundary cases.
template noop identity vs formatting behavior.

related_files.relationship: Implements the behavior under test.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any encoding/validation changes must be compatible with these strict expectations.

insights.title: Documented deterministic source-link ref resolution order for RTD and local builds
insights.problem: Contributors lacked clear guidance on how docs source links decide which Git ref to use, especially outside a git checkout/RTD.
insights.root_cause: Docs source-link logic evolved from a `main` fallback to ref-aware resolution but the contributor-facing documentation didn’t keep pace.
insights.solution: Added a “Documentation and Read the Docs” section describing local build scripts, RTD configuration, and the ref-resolution order (`OCTODNS_SOURCE_BASE_URL`, `READTHEDOCS_GIT_IDENTIFIER`, local git context, no longer silent `main` fallback).
insights.lesson_learned: When doc build behavior changes (especially error vs fallback), update contributor docs with the exact resolution order and how to override (e.g., fork builds).

insights.title: Fix parsing regex for slash command flags and push target branch
insights.problem: The workflow failed to parse /changelog create arguments when using different flag formats (-t vs --type) and could push to the wrong branch target.
insights.root_cause: The sed regex only matched one flag format and the branch-detection logic was fragile (and removed SHA/branch detection entirely).
insights.solution: Update the sed regex to handle both -t and --type forms. Replace branch pushing logic with a deterministic push ref targeting the PR head ref: refs/pull/<issue_number>/head.
insights.lesson_learned: For CI workflows that react to PR/issue contexts, avoid brittle branch guessing. Prefer GitHub-provided ref targets and make command parsing robust to supported flag variants.

insights.title: Switch requirements.txt management to proviso
insights.problem: Maintaining requirements via a custom Python script was becoming complex and brittle (especially with Python-version-specific pins and extras).
insights.root_cause: The update-requirements script implemented custom freezing/version-selection logic that was hard to maintain and duplicative of tooling.
insights.solution: Replace the script implementation with a thin wrapper around 'proviso' for deterministic requirements management: `proviso --header=... "$@"`.
insights.lesson_learned: When dependency management logic is non-trivial (extras, environment markers, version pin overrides), use a dedicated tool rather than maintaining bespoke freeze/merge logic.

insights.title: Bootstrap no longer installs docs requirements-dev.txt split removed
insights.problem: Documentation dependencies were no longer installed during non-production bootstrap.
insights.root_cause: The script changed from installing requirements-dev.txt and requirements-docs.txt together to only installing requirements-dev.txt when ENV != production.
insights.solution: Remove requirements-docs.txt installation from bootstrap; presumably docs requirements are handled elsewhere.
insights.lesson_learned: If your CI/build expects docs tooling dependencies, ensure they are installed by the correct dedicated workflow/step. Removing them from bootstrap can break doc builds if not compensated.

insights.title: New tests validate root_ns_warnings + strict_supports behavior
insights.problem: Root NS behavior had multiple conditional branches and could easily regress.
insights.root_cause: Complex log/exception behavior based on provider support flags and configured records.
insights.solution: Added `test_root_ns_warnings` verifying: warning appears when SUPPORTS_ROOT_NS=True but desired.root_ns is missing; no warning when root_ns_warnings=False; warning when SUPPORTS_ROOT_NS=False but existing root NS configured; and SupportsException when strict_supports=True.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding config toggles that alter logging or exceptions, include test cases for both default and disabled states and for strict mode.

insights.title: Add tests to validate Zone.apply works on copies and matches target
insights.problem: Without direct tests for Zone.apply, provider-level behavior changes could be introduced without confidence in the underlying state transition.
insights.root_cause: Zone.apply did not previously exist; mutation behavior was only implicitly verified via providers/managers.
insights.solution: Added tests ensuring `copy.apply([])` preserves records and that applying a non-empty `changes` set mutates the copy to match the expected target records while diverging from the original.
insights.lesson_learned: When extracting/shared logic, add focused unit tests for the new primitive (Zone.apply) before relying on downstream consumers.

insights.title: Ensure YAML tmp dir is reset between multiple Manager.sync calls
insights.problem: Tests that repeatedly sync using the same temporary YAML output directory could become order-dependent due to leftover `.yaml` artifacts between runs.
insights.root_cause: Temporary output directory was not cleared between successive sync operations in the same test, leading to persistent state influencing later assertions.
insights.solution: Introduced a `reset(directory)` helper to delete generated `.yaml` files and called it before each relevant sync in `TestManager` (including targeted and non-targeted sync sections).
insights.lesson_learned: When testing file-based providers with shared temp directories, always reset/clean outputs between independent assertions to avoid hidden coupling and flaky tests.

insights.title: Test coverage for nested env var expansion at 1st/2nd/3rd levels
insights.problem: Without tests at multiple nesting depths, future regressions could reintroduce non-recursive expansion or incorrect type conversion.
insights.root_cause: Coverage previously focused on top-level expansion only.
insights.solution:
Added TestManager.test_build_kwargs, validating:
- empty input
- no expansion for direct values
- top-level env/OCTODNS_TEST_* expansion with numeric coercion for '42'
- recursive expansion at 2nd and 3rd nesting levels and preservation of non-env values
insights.lesson_learned: When adding recursive config processing, test representative nesting depths and type outcomes (e.g., numeric conversion).

insights.title: Adjust tests to align with updated YamlProvider.populate semantics and plan idempotency
insights.problem: Provider tests assumed earlier semantics that With target, records would be ignored (and that a second sync would still create records). Those assumptions no longer held after changing populate/apply semantics.
insights.root_cause: Test expectations were coupled to old provider behavior; changes made provider return/plan behavior more idempotent and accurate.
insights.solution: Updated assertions so that when using `target=source`, the provider still populates records (counts updated). Also changed expectations that a second sync produces no plan (`self.assertFalse(plan)`), and added handling for idna/utf8 file presence to ensure plan generation returns nothing when file contents match.
insights.lesson_learned: When provider semantics are corrected (especially idempotency and mode-dependent behavior), update tests to assert the new invariants: correct record visibility and no-op plans on repeated runs.

insights.title: Added/updated tests for Record.copy with no values (values: [])
insights.problem: No explicit test ensured Record.copy behaved correctly when values is an empty list.
insights.root_cause: Missing boundary test for empty values list.
insights.solution: Added test_record_copy_with_no_values which creates a TXT record with values: [] (lenient=True) then verifies dup.values matches and does not crash/mis-handle.
insights.lesson_learned: Add explicit boundary tests for empty collections, especially for copy/serialization logic.

insights.title: Test coverage for Record.data including octodns metadata and deep copy isolation
insights.problem: The octodns subsection being omitted from Record.data could go unnoticed without tests.
insights.root_cause: No tests validated that record.data/copy preserve internal octodns metadata with deep-copy semantics.
insights.solution:
Added test_record_octodns_with_data_and_copy verifying:
- data equality between original and copy
- modifying returned b.data does not mutate original
- direct mutations of _octodns (first-level and deeper) affect data
This confirms both inclusion and deep copy isolation.
insights.lesson_learned: When exposing internal metadata via serialization (data), tests should validate both presence and deep-copy behavior to prevent accidental shared references.

insights.title: Tests updated to reflect normalized ValuesMixin data output for empty values
insights.problem: Previous tests expected absence of keys in certain empty cases, but new serialization guarantees included explicit empty collections.
insights.root_cause: ValuesMixin._data behavior changed to always represent empty values consistently.
insights.solution:
